worked: Data-first inversion was the right call: no-truncation, deep search, and cheap incremental live all fell out of one architecture change. Pagination (vs virtualization) made it simpler still — only ~50 DOM nodes at a time, no `content-visibility` quirks.

Verifying against the REAL 10 MB file at each step caught the 3× token bug and surfaced the duplicate-turn structure. Headless Chrome DOM probes (`--dump-dom` + injected probe writing JSON into a hidden `#probe` div) gave deterministic UI verification; screenshots confirmed pixels.
gotchas: One assistant `message.id` spans MULTIPLE JSONL lines (one per content block: thinking/text/tool_use), with `usage` repeated identically on each — never sum usage per line.

Sub-agent transcripts are separate files under `<session>/subagents/`; the parent has `isSidechain:false` and no inline sidechain content in this CC version. Join key is `toolUseId`. Some sessions' metas LACK `toolUseId` (notion-sync: 9 of 13) → count from the file list, not the join map.

`content-visibility: auto` renders BLANK in headless single-shot screenshots (no scroll/compositor pass) — looks broken but isn't; real browsers paint on scroll. This is why I moved off it (plus the user's UX complaint).
Headless `--dump-dom` ignores `--window-size` (defaults ~800px); pass it to the `--screenshot` invocation, judge full-width by ratio not absolute px.
feedback: Don't drop existing features during a rewrite — inventory them as a parity checklist and flag any removal. (ribbon regression)

Don't implement the literal wording of a request; model its INTENT. "Highlight" ≠ "replace the fill"; "max 20/row" ≠ "cap the width"; "improve the pager" ≠ "rearrange the same buttons." (cluster-C atone)
The user wants real debate/depth, not cosmetic motion called improvement.

pending: **Phase 5 (task #6, pending):** delete the ~1,450-line legacy HTML generator in `detail.sh` (the heredoc before the `cp`), remove the now-dead 800 KB seek + `messages[-100:]` cap, drop the old `data-search` path, update file header comments, and write a migration note under `~/.claude/migrations/` (structural change to a canonical widget). **HELD until the user confirms the new viewer in a real browser** — do not delete the legacy path before that.

**Phase 4 (task #5, pending but largely DONE in code):** sub-agent cards, lazy drill-in (`/data?agent=`), per-turn telemetry, typed events are all implemented in `transcript-app.html`. Remaining/optional: exercise the drill-in click in a live browser; add inter-turn duration + cumulative cost; surface the 9 unlinked sub-agents (count is fixed, but metas without `toolUseId` still can't be drilled into).

**Deferred from skeptical-review:** `/data` has no mtime cache — re-parses the full file each poll (~64 ms now, grows with size). 40% perf smell, not correctness. Add a `(path, mtime)`-keyed parse cache if polling cost matters.

**Search occurrence/mark mismatch** — guarded (falls back to first mark) but not fully fixed: occurrence count is over raw text, highlights are over rendered markdown DOM. Proper fix = count over post-render text.
**Restart the live server** so the user can see it (offered, not yet done).
